subject: Statistics
title: Additive vs. Multiplicative Decomposition
description: A quick, practical guide to Additive vs. Multiplicative Decomposition.

hook.text: Retail sales swing by $10M each December in 2010 and $50M in 2023. The swing grew. That's multiplicative.

buildup.text: Additive decomposition: Data = Trend + Season + Residual. The seasonal effect is a fixed amount.

discovery.text: Multiplicative: Data = Trend × Season × Residual. The seasonal effect is proportional to the trend level.

twist.text: Choosing wrong distorts everything. A log transform converts multiplicative into additive — a common trick.

climax.text: Most economic and business data is multiplicative — bigger values have bigger swings. Nature prefers ratios.

punchline.text: Fixed swings are additive. Proportional swings are multiplicative.

quiz.question: When should you use multiplicative rather than additive decomposition?
options: When seasonal swings grow proportionally with the trend level
When the data has no trend
When there is no seasonal pattern
